rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to ports or features visible at that level.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for all AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Input envelope assumptions should be placed in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Output guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs without using implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Ensure type alignment between helper declarations and expressions in AGREE contracts.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq declarations for boolean range checks: eq var : bool = value >= min and value <= max;
Write guarantees using visible feature names and valid AGREE expressions only.
Component type contracts must be placed in component type annexes, not implementations.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables.
Do not attempt to encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE variables
Implementation-level bindings and assignments in component implementations
